title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Go Client Systems · Execution Trace: Status Families
summary: A package-signing client must distinguish protocol failure, rejected policy, malformed problem details, and an unknown write outcome. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go status families increment in a Go 1.26 net/http typed response and failure classifier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Interpret charset and language metadata, negotiation quality values, and Unicode boundaries without locale guesses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
